reference_answer: Assurance No. 24-099; brought by Letitia James, Attorney General of the State of New York, through the Bureau of Internet and Technology.
Equinox Group, LLC; Equinox Holdings, Inc.; Equinox Media, LLC; and SoulCycle LLC.
A Delaware corporation; its principal place of business is at 31 Hudson Yards, New York, NY 10001.
Blink Fitness; it filed for bankruptcy on August 12, 2024.
Executive Law § 63(12); General Business Law §§ 349, 350, and 527-a; and the Restore Online Shoppers' Confidence Act (ROSCA), 15 U.S.C. § 8403(3).
There are currently 43 Equinox gyms in New York state — more than in any other state.
SoulCycle Inc., 609 Greenwich Street, New York, New York, 10014.
Since at least February 9, 2021 and until at least July 28, 2024.
September 2021.
Beginning July 28, 2024.
On the 23rd day of each month.
At least 45 days of notice.
March 2023.

(i) failing to present the material terms of the offer clearly and conspicuously before the subscription was fulfilled; (ii) failing to obtain the consumer's affirmative consent to the agreement containing the automatic renewal offer terms; and (iii) failing to provide a cost-effective, timely, and easy-to-use mechanism for cancellation. GBL § 527-a(1)-(2) (and ROSCA, 15 U.S.C. § 8403).

(i) a physical disability that would prevent use of Equinox's services for more than six months; (ii) moving more than 25 miles away from any Equinox location; (iii) Equinox could no longer provide the contracted services (e.g., their location closed); and (iv) death, in which case the estate could cancel.
"Select" (access to only a single gym); "All-Access" (access to all U.S. Equinox locations); and "Destination" (access to Equinox locations worldwide).

"In larger type than the surrounding text, or in contrasting type, font, or color to the surrounding text of the same size, or set off from the surrounding text of the same size by symbols or other marks, in a manner that clearly calls attention to the language."
Within three days of joining.

Among the material terms: the classes expired after 30 days and did not roll over into the next month; the classes were non-transferable; and a Member had to cancel at least 24 hours prior to the next billing cycle to avoid being charged for the following month (others include the price depending on number of classes, automatic monthly renewal until cancellation, SoulCycle's reserved right to incrementally increase the price upon advance written notice, and the billing date).
The consumer's express affirmative consent to the negative option feature of the membership.

The initiation fee (which could be as high as $300) was disclosed for the first time on the payment page, only after the consumer had navigated three other pages — and after the enrollment flow had already obtained the consumer's consent to be contacted by a Membership Advisor.

Managers were directed to "TRY TO SAVE THE MEMBER" (e.g., offer to freeze or transfer the membership) and were instructed that they should not actively inform Members that they might be able to cancel without further charge in certain circumstances; the instruction stated that if a member is suffering from a medical condition or relocating, "this is something that will come up in conversation and is not something we need to offer."
The online webchat cancellation option (cancellation via webchat with an Equinox Concierge) — it was not included in the membership documents or FAQs.

The webchat cancellation required the Member to respond to between 1 and 4 automated prompts, wait to connect to a Concierge, and respond to at least one "save" attempt before the cancellation process would begin — significantly more burdensome than the enrollment flow, which required navigating through just three pages and guided the consumer with prominent visual and textual cues.

That the 20% discount applied only to the consumer's initial purchase and that subsequent packages would automatically renew at the higher, standard price (i.e., when the promotion would end). This fact was not disclosed anywhere — not in the promotional offer, not in the online purchase flow, and not in the terms and conditions that appeared in the purchase flow.
